title: scabiosa turolensis subsp. weyleri
intro: Scabiosa turolensis subsp. weyleri, also known as the Weyler's scabious, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the Iberian Peninsula. It is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the honeysuckle family (Caprifoliaceae) and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its health benefits, such as wound healing and the treatment of respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Scabiosa turolensis subsp. weyleri can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
